macro_summary: PSX KSE-100 holds above 180,000 (+887 pts intraday); US-Iran memorandum electronically signed with Pakistan as mediator; Hormuz reopening expected to depress oil prices (negative read for E&P, mildly positive for OMCs and macro inflation); current account posts $459m surplus in May; rupee firm; ADB $700m loan approved for the insurance sector.

OGDC.summary: Two same-day negatives: Pakistan missed FY26 oil & gas production targets as drilling fell short; Hormuz reopening expected to release oil supply and depress prices, pressuring E&P realisations.
headlines: Pakistan misses oil, gas production targets as drilling falls short in FY26
Hormuz reopening to release wave of oil supply, depress prices

PPL.summary: Same FY26 oil & gas production shortfall hits PPL volumes; Hormuz-reopening oil supply wave adds price headwind.
headlines: Pakistan misses oil, gas production targets as drilling falls short in FY26
Hormuz reopening to release wave of oil supply, depress prices

SNGP.summary: FY26 gas production miss flows through to SNGPL throughput. No offsetting catalyst this 24h window.
headlines: Pakistan misses oil, gas production targets as drilling falls short in FY26

SSGC.summary: FY26 production miss balanced by ECC approval of gas supply for National Steel Complex revival — modest fresh demand tailwind.
headlines: Pakistan misses oil, gas production targets as drilling falls short in FY26
ECC approves gas supply for National Steel Complex revival

PSO.summary: Govt signalling petrol price cut as global oil eases (Hormuz-reopening supply wave) — typically widens OMC volumes; thinner unit margins are the offset.
headlines: Govt signals petroleum price cut as global oil rates ease
Hormuz reopening to release wave of oil supply, depress prices

AIRLINK.summary: Subsidiary Select Technologies IPO opens with book building June 22-23 — concrete dates lock in the AC-manufacturing capacity catalyst into next week.
headlines: Select Technologies IPO opens, book building June 22-23

PIBTL.summary: Port Qasim Authority renewing the Engro Vopak terminal implementation agreement validates terminal-sector longevity at the port — a read-across positive for PIBTL's own concession outlook.
headlines: Port Qasim Authority renews Engro Vopak Terminal implementation agreement
